sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of the bcdedit command to tamper with the boot configuration data. This technique is often times used by malware or attackers as a destructive way before launching ransomware.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may disable or modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage
A General detection for files being created in the Windows startup directory. This could be an indicator of persistence.
Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall

values.CommandLine: netsh  advfirewall set currentprofile state off

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Hidden Executable In NTFS Alternate Data Stream
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an ADS (Alternate Data Stream) that contains an executable by looking at a non-empty Imphash
Detects execution of netsh with the "advfirewall" and the "set" option in order to set new values for properties of a existing rule
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: PowerShell Module File Created By Non-PowerShell Process
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of a new PowerShell module ".psm1", ".psd1", ".dll", ".ps1", etc. by a non-PowerShell process

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detect Eking ransomware using its rich header
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Sekoia.io

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This sample is a network worm with multiple propagation and defense evasion capabilities. It scans the local network for open SMB ports (TCP/445) and enumerates network shares using WNet APIs to spread laterally. It also copies itself to other drives, likely including removable ones. The malware establishes persistence by creating registry Run keys. For privilege escalation, it employs a UAC bypass technique, stealing the access token from the shell process using `DuplicateTokenEx`. Additionally, it can enumerate and terminate other processes (`TerminateProcess`), likely to disable security software. C2 communication is performed via HTTP POST requests using `WinHttpSendRequest`.
